findings.title: Real path-safety failure path is untested
findings.impact: Command.run now depends on util.valid_safe_path rejecting unsafe config paths, but the tests replace that helper with an identity lambda. A regression that stops converting ValueError from the real path guard into FAILURE would still pass the current suite, even though unsafe operator input is one of the new command's file-load failure modes.
findings.fix: Add a Command.run test that uses the real cli.common.util.valid_safe_path behavior, or a stub that raises ValueError for an unsafe path, and assert the command returns FAILURE.

findings.title: Invalid-config reporting is not asserted
findings.impact: The PR's main CLI behavior is to report every structural problem before deploy, but test_run_invalid_config_returns_failure only checks the return code. If this loop were removed or only the first problem were logged, the command would stop giving operators the promised complete error list while all current tests still passed.
findings.fix: Capture the command logger for an invalid config with multiple problems and assert that each expected problem is emitted, not just that the return code is FAILURE.
